transformations: subtract baseline and singleton recoveries from pair recovery

visible_condition.plain_language: A bridge appears only when two declared views are opened together.

available_affordances.purpose: Open projection A and projection B together and compare the pair residue with baseline and both singletons.

route_topology.residue_rule: pair - union(baseline, singleton A, singleton B)

research_frame.question: Can a participant identify a relation that depends on the pair without inflating it into identity, intent, or causation?
research_frame.hypothesis: Sequentially exposing baseline, both singletons, and the pair will support a bounded pair-emergence explanation.
observable_behavior: participant verifies the relation is absent from baseline
participant verifies the relation is absent from each singleton
participant describes the bridge as pair-dependent rather than universally present
alternative_explanations: the pair-only bridge may result from incomplete singleton observation
the residue may reflect a projection-order or encoding artifact
expected_failure_modes: pair residue is narrated as causation
missing singleton evidence is treated as absence
the bridge receives celebratory authority styling
falsifier: the bridge appears in baseline or either singleton
the pair result lacks the declared bridge
a held-out control produces the same residue under unrelated projections
abstention_conditions: either singleton is missing or unresolved
the pair observation is missing or unresolved

missingness: held-out control not included in this minimal fixture
contradictions: each singleton is locally complete for its own view while the pair exposes an additional relation

allowed_claims: the declared pair contains a residue absent from the supplied baseline and singletons

causal_trace: the pair-only bridge remained after subtraction
contradictions: pair information exceeds the union of supplied singleton recoveries
unresolved_relations: whether the bridge survives a held-out control

static_equivalent.summary: Four labeled panes show baseline, A, B, and A+B, with the pair-only bridge outlined and the subtraction rule visible.

name.plain_language: The relation appears only when these two views are held together.

context.example: two model projections reveal a shared residual

transfer_context.shared_relation_candidate: a relation becomes observable only through a declared comparison
observable_correspondence: the compared pair exposes a residual absent from each isolated view
alternative_explanations: a missing isolated observation could imitate pair emergence
abstention_conditions: any required isolated comparison is unresolved
